Engage in daily physical activities, such as walking, and maintain control over blood pressure, blood

eggplant, and turnips.
Choose lean proteins like poultry, fish, eggs, and unsalted seafood; drink water and unsweetened tea;
consume fish rich in omega-3 fatty acids.

Counseling for obesity :
Obesity is a complex disease involving an excessive amount of body fat. Obesity isn't just a cosmetic
concern.
It's a medical problem that increases the risk of other diseases and health problems, such as heart
disease, diabetes, high blood pressure and certain cancers.
The good news is that even modest weight loss can improve or prevent the health problems
associated with obesity.
A healthier diet, increased physical activity and behavior changes can help you lose weight.

Date of Service:
EXAM: CT SOFT TISSUE NECK WITH CONTRAST
HISTORY: Nodule of the pharynx.
TECHNIQUE: Contiguous axial images were obtained utilizing a multislice, multidetector CT scanner. Post-
processed reformations were also submitted for review. The images were obtained following the administration
of IV contrast.
Contrast: 100 cc Optiray 350 IV contrast.
The total DLP was 671.31 mGy-cm and the CTDI was 20.48 mGy.
One or more of the following dose reduction techniques were used: automated exposure control, adjustment of
the mA and/or kV according to patient size, use of iterative reconstruction technique. A total of 1 CT
(Computed Tomography) examinations and 0 myocardial perfusion studies have been performed on this patient
over the past 12 months. Counts as indicated include examinations performed within our network.
COMPARISON: CT chest from 2/19/2026.
FINDINGS: Superficial right parotid calcification. Parotid and submandibular glands are otherwise normal.
Unremarkable appearance of the thyroid gland. 36 X 30 X 48 mm enhancing mass in the left oropharynx at the
base of the tongue with extension across the midline to the right. Mass abuts the epiglottis and left hyoid.
Centrally hypodense 27 X 35 X 38 mm right level 2A mass. Normal opacification of the neck vessels. Cervical
spondylosis with spinal canal and neural foraminal stenosis at C5-C6 and C6-C7. No aggressive osseous
lesions. No intracranial abnormality. Imaged paranasal sinuses, mastoid air cells, and orbits are unremarkable.
IMPRESSION:
Large left tongue base mass measuring 27 X 35 X 38 mm with extension across the midline to the right,
squamous cell carcinoma until proven otherwise.
Presumed metastatic right level 2A lymph node.
Cervical spondylosis.
Confidential
Patient:
Page 1 of 2

claude_vision.referral_reason: Urgent ENT consultation for a large left tongue base mass with extension across the midline, suspected squamous cell carcinoma, with presumed metastatic right level 2A lymph node. Patient presented with hemoptysis (coughing up blood).
relevant_clinical_findings: Chief complaint: Follow-up and coughing up blood (hemoptysis)

CT neck with contrast revealed: 36 x 30 x 48 mm enhancing mass in the left oropharynx at the base of the tongue with extension across the midline to the right; mass abuts the epiglottis and left hyoid
Centrally hypodense 27 x 35 x 38 mm right level 2A mass — presumed metastatic lymph node
CT impression: Large left tongue base mass measuring 27 x 35 x 38 mm, squamous cell carcinoma until proven otherwise
Diagnosis coded as D37.02 — Neoplasm of uncertain behavior of base of tongue
History of tonsillectomy (removed tonsils before age 12)
Allergy to Epinephrine — clinically relevant for ENT procedures requiring local anesthetic with vasoconstrictors
OSA (obstructive sleep apnea) — relevant given mass abutting epiglottis and potential airway implications
Patient reports anxiousness and depressed mood — relevant in context of cancer workup
Referral triaged directly by supervising MD (Dr. Hasday) with same-day scheduling instruction: 'I have an opening at 1pm tomorrow. Call him in AM and have him come in please.'
claude_vision.imaging_summary: CT Soft Tissue Neck with Contrast performed at DRI Elk Grove (RadNet), ordered by Dr. Paramjit Takhar. Key findings: (1) Large enhancing mass in the left oropharynx at the base of the tongue measuring 36 x 30 x 48 mm, extending across the midline to the right, abutting the epiglottis and left hyoid — impression is squamous cell carcinoma until proven otherwise. (2) Centrally hypodense 27 x 35 x 38 mm right level 2A mass — presumed metastatic lymph node. (3) Cervical spondylosis with spinal canal and neural foraminal stenosis at C5-C6 and C6-C7. (4) No aggressive osseous lesions. CT report IS attached (pages 13 of fax). Comparison study referenced: CT chest from 2/19/2026 — chest CT report is NOT included in this fax.

provider_urgency_label.label: urgent

(2) Referral Information table on page 04 lists 'Priority: Emergency' and 'Status Reason: Care Ctr/Clinic Action Needed'. matched_criteria: Rapidly growing neck or oral lesions
evidence: CT impression: 'Large left tongue base mass measuring 27 x 35 x 38 mm with extension across the midline to the right, squamous cell carcinoma until proven otherwise' — directly meets suspected malignancy criterion

CT findings: '36 x 30 x 48 mm enhancing mass in the left oropharynx at the base of the tongue' with 'presumed metastatic right level 2A lymph node' — large mass with suspected nodal spread meets rapidly growing/advanced oral lesion criterion
CT findings: 'Mass abuts the epiglottis and left hyoid' — proximity to the epiglottis raises concern for impending airway compromise, meeting airway obstruction criterion
Diagnosis D37.02: Neoplasm of uncertain behavior of base of tongue — coded as primary encounter diagnosis
Referral reason explicitly states: '**URGENT** D37.02 (ICD-10-CM) - Neoplasm of uncertain behavior of base of tongue'
criteria_check.provider_label: Urgent / Emergency — labeled '**URGENT**' on referral cover letter, 'Priority: Emergency' in referral information table, and triaged directly by supervising MD with same-day scheduling instruction
criteria_check.reasoning: This referral is FLAGGED FOR PRIORITY REVIEW because the clinical content independently matches three urgent ENT criteria — suspected malignancy, a large and potentially rapidly progressing oropharyngeal mass, and epiglottic abutment raising airway concern — and the referring provider's urgency labeling (Emergency/Urgent) is fully consistent with and corroborated by the clinical findings.

claude_vision.summary: This is an urgent ENT referral for a male patient with a large left tongue base mass (up to 48mm) extending across the midline and abutting the epiglottis, with a presumed metastatic right level 2A lymph node, identified on CT neck with contrast — impression is squamous cell carcinoma until proven otherwise. The patient presented with hemoptysis and has multiple comorbidities including Type 2 diabetes, CKD, obesity, OSA, and a history of CVA; the OSA and epiglottic involvement are particularly relevant given airway risk. The referring provider labeled this Emergency/Urgent, a supervising MD (Dr. Hasday) personally triaged the case for next-day scheduling, and the clinical findings independently meet multiple ENT urgent criteria — this referral should be treated as high priority with prompt scheduling for evaluation, likely including laryngoscopy and biopsy coordination with oncology.
